---
title: "User and Workdir"
description: "Control the default sandbox user and working directory from Go templates."
---

# User and Workdir

Current E2B templates default to the `user` account with `/home/user` as the working directory. This differs from plain Docker defaults, which usually start as `root` in `/`.

## Go-specific behavior

- `SetUser(...)` and `SetWorkdir(...)` are template builder steps on `*e2b.TemplateBase`
- the last values you set become the default execution user and working directory for sandboxes created from that template
- `RunCmd(..., &struct{ User string }{...})` changes only that build step; use `SetUser(...)` if you want to change the built sandbox default
- after the sandbox exists, one-off runtime overrides use `sandbox.Commands.Run(ctx, cmd, &e2b.CommandStartOpts{User: ..., Cwd: ...})`

## Default sandbox user and workdir

Without any template customization, sandbox commands run as `user` in `/home/user`.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()

	sandbox, _ := e2b.Create(ctx, "", nil)

	whoamiExec, _ := sandbox.Commands.Run(ctx, "whoami", nil)
	pwdExec, _ := sandbox.Commands.Run(ctx, "pwd", nil)

	whoami := whoamiExec.(*e2b.CommandResult)
	pwd := pwdExec.(*e2b.CommandResult)

	_ = whoami.Stdout
	_ = pwd.Stdout
}
```

## Custom user and workdir in a template

Use `SetUser(...)` and `SetWorkdir(...)` to persist new defaults into the built template.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()

	template := e2b.Template(nil).
		FromBaseImage().
		RunCmd("whoami").
		RunCmd("pwd").
		SetUser("guest").
		SetWorkdir("/home/guest").
		RunCmd("whoami").
		RunCmd("pwd")

	_, _ = e2b.Build(ctx, template, "custom-user-template", &e2b.BuildOptions{
		BasicBuildOptions: e2b.BasicBuildOptions{
			OnBuildLogs: e2b.DefaultBuildLogger(),
		},
	})

	sandbox, _ := e2b.Create(ctx, "custom-user-template", nil)

	whoamiExec, _ := sandbox.Commands.Run(ctx, "whoami", nil)
	pwdExec, _ := sandbox.Commands.Run(ctx, "pwd", nil)

	whoami := whoamiExec.(*e2b.CommandResult)
	pwd := pwdExec.(*e2b.CommandResult)

	_ = whoami.Stdout
	_ = pwd.Stdout
}
```

If you only need elevated privileges during the build, keep that override on the individual step:

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	template := e2b.Template(nil).
		FromBaseImage().
		RunCmd("apt-get update", &struct{ User string }{User: "root"}).
		RunCmd("apt-get install -y curl", &struct{ User string }{User: "root"}).
		SetUser("user").
		SetWorkdir("/home/user")

	_ = template
}
```

## Override user or workdir at runtime

After a sandbox is running, you can still override user or working directory for a single command without changing the template defaults.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	sandbox, _ := e2b.Connect(ctx, "sbx_123", nil)

	asRootExec, _ := sandbox.Commands.Run(ctx, "whoami", &e2b.CommandStartOpts{
		User: "root",
	})
	inTmpExec, _ := sandbox.Commands.Run(ctx, "pwd", &e2b.CommandStartOpts{
		Cwd: "/tmp",
	})

	asRoot := asRootExec.(*e2b.CommandResult)
	inTmp := inTmpExec.(*e2b.CommandResult)

	_ = asRoot.Stdout
	_ = inTmp.Stdout
}
```

For the full template builder surface, see the [Go SDK Template reference](/docs/sdk-reference/go-sdk/template). For runtime command options, see the [Go SDK Sandbox Commands reference](/docs/sdk-reference/go-sdk/sandbox-commands).
